topics.label: Warning
topics.recapText: I already warned you: something foul stirs on the eastern road. Keep your wits about you.

greeting.speakerText: Well met, stranger. The roads are long and the world is wide, but here we are, standing in the same spot. Funny how that works.

return_greeting.speakerText: Ah, you again. The road keeps bringing us together. Must be fate, or just a very small world.

tales_entry.speakerText: You want stories? I've got plenty. Every trail leaves its mark, and I've walked more trails than most people know exist.

responses.displayText: Tell me about the mountain pass.

tales_mountain_response.speakerText: The Greypeak Pass. Three days of thin air and narrow ledges, with nothing but cloud below you and stone above. Beautiful and terrible in equal measure.

responses.displayText: What dangers did you face up there?
What could you see from the top?

tales_mountain_dangers_response.speakerText: Rock slides are the obvious threat, but the real danger is what lives in the crevices. There's a shrine hidden up there, old stone covered in script nobody can read. Something guards it. I heard chanting on the wind and didn't stay to find out more.

tales_mountain_scenery_response.speakerText: On a clear morning, you can see all the way to the Amber Coast. The whole world laid out beneath you like a painted map. For a moment, you forget how cold your fingers are.

tales_desert_response.speakerText: Five days across the Sunhallow Flats with two waterskins and a prayer. The sand plays tricks on your eyes: towers that aren't there, rivers that vanish when you run toward them. I made it across, but only just.

tales_coast_response.speakerText: The Amber Coast road winds along the cliffs. Salt spray, crying gulls, the smell of brine. Peaceful, if you don't mind the smugglers. They use the sea caves to move goods at night. Polite enough, so long as you don't ask questions.

trade_entry.speakerText: I picked up a few things on the road that I don't need anymore. Might be useful to someone with purpose, though. Interested?

trade_map_response.speakerText: A hand-drawn map of the northern valleys. Every trail marker, every water source. Took me two seasons to chart it all. Treat it well.

trade_compass_response.speakerText: This compass has seen me through fog and forest alike. The needle's got a slight wobble near lodestone, but otherwise she points true. Yours now.

trade_rations_response.speakerText: Dried meat, hardtack, and a pouch of salt. Won't win any feasts, but it'll keep you walking for a week. Better than an empty stomach on a cold trail.

warning_entry.speakerText: Listen: the eastern road past the mill is not safe. I came through there two nights ago and heard things in the treeline. Not animals. Something deliberate, something patient. I don't scare easily, but I did not sleep that night.

shrine_entry.speakerText: So you've heard about the shrine in the Greypeak Pass. Not many people know it's there, and fewer still have the nerve to go looking. I can tell you what I saw, if you're serious about finding it.

responses.displayText: What do you think the chanting meant?

shrine_details_response.speakerText: It's past the second switchback, where the path narrows to single file. Look for a crack in the rock face on your left, barely wide enough to squeeze through. The shrine is inside: a carved stone altar with symbols that seem to shift when you look away. The air tastes like copper there.

shrine_insight_pass.speakerText: You've got sharp instincts. The chanting wasn't a warning: it was an invitation. Whatever dwells there wants to be found, but only by someone who understands what they're walking into. Be ready for a bargain, not a fight.

shrine_insight_fail.speakerText: Hard to say. Could be spirits, could be the wind playing tricks in those narrow caves. I wouldn't read too much into it. Just be careful if you go up there.

directions_entry.speakerText: Directions? Sure, I know this region well enough. Where are you trying to get to?

responses.displayText: How do I reach the nearest town?

directions_town_response.speakerText: Follow the main road south for half a day. You'll pass a stone bridge over a dry creek bed: keep left at the fork after that. You'll smell the bakery before you see the walls.

directions_river_response.speakerText: Head east until you hit the treeline, then follow it north. The crossing is where the river bends around a stand of white birch. Shallow enough to wade if the rains haven't come.

directions_mark_map_response.speakerText: Here, let me see. Right: you're here, the town is there, and the river crossing is about... there. I've marked the safe trails. Stick to those and you'll be fine.
